login.title: Login
login.cookie-p: GeoHealth only uses cookies strictly necessary for its proper functioning. No other cookies are used.

home.summary: GeoHealth, a research group at the University of Namur specializing in spatial epidemiology and modeling, studies infectious diseases using the One Health approach, which links human, animal, and environmental health.
home.text1: Multi-layer WebGIS with zoom, filters, and visualization of risk maps and associated factors.
home.text2: Risk assessment by department with visualization, export, and anonymized collaborative tracking
home.text3: Integration of new georeferenced datasets and recalculation using the provided R scripts.
home.text4: Identification of the leading risk factors and associated preventive measures by department.
home.problem: Zoonoses account for 75% of emerging infectious diseases such as Ebola and COVID-19. To prepare for these diseases, it is essential to have reliable, validated risk maps in order to better guide interventions and strengthen the public health response.

profile.password-message: You want to change your password ?
profile.account-second-title: Please type and validate your password to confirm

navigation.try-another: Try another title or clear the search.

evalmodal.title: Evaluation for a division
evalmodal.firstdescription: Indicate your degree of agreement between the modelled average risk index and the actual risk as you perceive it in the field. Please answer on a scale of 1 to 4: 1 = strongly disagree 2 = somewhat disagree 3 = somewhat agree 4 = strongly agree
evalmodal.seconddescription: Indicate your level of certainty regarding your own assessment. In other words, how confident are you in your answer? Please answer on a scale of 1 to 4: 1 = very uncertain 2 = somewhat uncertain 3 = somewhat certain 4 = very certain
evalmodal.thirddescription: Please indicate the risk category you assign to this department based on your perception of the field, your expertise and the information available to you. Possible choices: low, medium, high.
evalmodal.fourthdescription: Click to make your evaluation visible to other users, and vice versa.
evalmodal.commentdescription: You can add a free comment to justify your evaluation or provide contextual details. For example: missing data, recent events, local knowledge, perceived biases, unusual changes in the situation, etc.

map.label: Click a Division
map.metric1description: Measures the level of agreement between evaluators for a given division. The higher the score, the more concordant the opinions.
map.metric2description: Measures the overall level of agreement between evaluators at the national scale. The higher the score, the stronger the consensus.
map.metric3description: National measure of consistency between evaluators for agreement score. The higher the value, the more consistent the judgments.
map.metric4description: Average of evaluators' agreement score with the model for a given division. The higher the score, the more consistent the model appears with field observations.
map.metric5description: Average certainty level of evaluators for a given division. The higher the score, the more confident they appear in their responses.
map.metric6description: Risk class that stands out the most from evaluators' responses, taking their certainty level into account.
map.metric7description: Measures the agreement between the model's risk class for a given division and the one perceived by evaluators, taking their certainty into account.
map.metric8description: National average of model-field agreement scores. The higher it is, the more the model globally corresponds to field observations.
